REFERENCE: The article by Greene and Riggs (2015) on generic insulin in the New England Journal of Medicine is a known, real publication. However, the citation is missing critical components: (1) the **journal name** (New England Journal of Medicine) is entirely absent, (2) the **volume number** (372) is missing, and (3) the number in parentheses '(12)' appears to be the issue number but is orphaned without surrounding context. The title, authors, year, page numbers (1171–1175), and DOI (10.1056/NEJMms1411398) are all consistent with the real article. The citation is therefore valid in substance but **incomplete and improperly formatted**.
